summary: Statistical methodology overhaul: Hedges' g correction, fix pseudo-replication, fix CI computation, apply multiple comparison corrections, fix unpaired method agreement
context: Statistical review identified critical issues: Cohen's d overestimated by ~19% at n=3, behavior-outcome correlations were pseudo-replicated, EffectSize CI was for one group's mean not the delta, method agreement in construct_validity correlated unpaired observations, and no multiple comparison correction on 20 correlations (64% false positive rate).
rationale: Applied Hedges' g bias correction, aggregated outcomes to cell means to fix pseudo-replication, switched to Spearman r for count data, computed bootstrap CI of mean difference for EffectSize, paired method agreement by ScoredResult, applied Holm-Bonferroni to all multi-test situations, renamed causal-sounding labels to descriptive ones.

alternatives.option: Leave statistical methods as-is with documentation caveats only
alternatives.reason_rejected: The pseudo-replication and unpaired correlations produce actively misleading results, not just imprecise ones. Documentation alone cannot fix fundamentally wrong computations.
